page: Google Cloud Platform a3-ultragpu-8g-nolssd is an accelerator-optimized server configured with eight NVIDIA H200 GPUs from the Hopper architecture, providing a total of 1123 GB of VRAM. The host system is powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor with 112 physical cores and 224 vCPUs operating at 2.1 GHz. It features 2952.0 GB of system memory, yielding approximately 13.18 GB of RAM per core. A key architectural tradeoff is the complete absence of local SSD storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached volumes.

The x86_64 CPU architecture includes dedicated allocation and a 260 MB L3 cache. It supports advanced instruction sets such as Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX, AVX512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), including amx_tile and amx_int8, which accelerate matrix operations. Cryptographic acceleration is supported via AES and SHA_NI flags.

In benchmark testing, this server demonstrates top-tier multi-core CPU performance, achieving a stress-ng multi-core score of 180184.5 and a Passmark CPU score of 125594.41. Memory subsystem performance is also in the top tier, with read-only cached small-block bandwidth reaching 13946.39 GB/sec and uncached large-block write bandwidth at 188.23 GB/sec. For database and memory-bound workloads, the system processed 221.56 million Redis SET operations per second. Large language model text generation benchmarks reached 32.58 tokens per second for a medium 7B model and 2.96 tokens per second for a large 70B model.

Given the high density of eight enterprise-grade GPUs and nearly three terabytes of RAM, this server represents a high-capital hardware class designed for massive parallel processing. The qualitative cost efficiency is centered on maximizing GPU-to-CPU resource density for large-scale workloads, though the lack of local storage requires external storage provisioning. This configuration is designed for large-scale AI and machine learning workloads, LLM training and inference, heavy database operations, and complex scientific simulations.
description: Google Cloud Platform a3-ultragpu-8g-nolssd is an accelerator-optimized server featuring eight NVIDIA H200 GPUs with 1123 GB of total VRAM, paired with 224 vCPUs on an Intel Xeon 8581C processor and 2952.0 GB of system memory. Built on the x86_64 architecture, the CPU includes AMX and AVX-512 extensions for accelerated matrix and vector calculations. This instance does not include local SSD storage, representing a clear tradeoff for workloads requiring high-speed local scratch space. In performance testing, the server achieves top-tier results in multi-core CPU execution, memory bandwidth, and database operations, processing 221.56 million Redis SET operations per second. It is engineered for large-scale parallel computing tasks, including deep learning, large language model inference, and intensive database workloads.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform a3-ultragpu-8g-nolssd features 8 NVIDIA H200 GPUs, 224 vCPUs, and 2952 GB RAM. It lacks local SSD storage and delivers top-tier multi-core and memory bandwidth performance.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform a3-ultragpu-8g-nolssd is a GPU-accelerated server with 8 NVIDIA H200 GPUs, 224 vCPUs, 2952 GB RAM, and no local SSD storage.
tagline: An accelerator-optimized platform combining eight H200 GPUs and massive system memory for parallel computing and large-scale AI workloads without local storage.
bullet_points: Eight NVIDIA H200 GPUs with 1123 GB of total VRAM
224 vCPUs via Intel Xeon 8581C with AMX and AVX-512 support
2952 GB of system memory providing high resource density
No local SSD storage included in the hardware configuration
Top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark performance
Best suited for large language model inference and parallel database operations
